mood.label: RISK-OFF
mood.summary: Markets are in a risk-off posture following a sharp tech-led selloff driven by AI spending concerns from Alphabet and Tesla. New broad US tariffs on 60 countries are further dampening sentiment, especially in Asia.

indicesNarrative: U.S. indices are pointing to a slightly higher open after Thursday's sharp sell-off, which saw the Nasdaq drop over 2%. The prior session's weakness was driven by heavy-volume selling in megacaps like Tesla and Alphabet on fears of runaway AI spending. Breadth was negative, with decliners outpacing advancers, and the VIX spiked, signaling rising investor fear.
technicalsNarrative: Technical data for individual indices is not available at this time. Analysis of entry risk, momentum, and key moving averages cannot be performed.

sectorsThemes.note: Dragged down by TSLA's sharp drop.

sectorsThemes.name: Technology
sectorsThemes.note: Broadly weak following AI spending concerns.

sectorsThemes.name: Utilities
sectorsThemes.note: Strong defensive bid amid market turmoil.

sectorsThemes.name: Energy
sectorsThemes.note: Volatile with oil prices, but showing relative strength for the week.

sectorsNarrative: Thursday saw a clear rotation out of growth sectors like Technology and Consumer Discretionary. Defensive sectors such as Utilities saw inflows as investors sought safety from the tech wreck.

movers.why: Missed profit estimates and guidance for a 'massive capex year' spooked investors about returns on AI spending.
Despite beating earnings, the stock fell sharply after raising its capital expenditure forecast for AI investment.
Stock surged after reporting strong Q2 results and positive commentary on its earnings call.
Stock fell after the wireless carrier's Q2 results missed revenue expectations.
Fell in pre-market after warning of future margin pressure from freight costs and tariffs, despite record revenue.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: Market in correction. No long setups meet criteria. Cash is the correct position.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: Clear rotation out of tech/growth into defensive names like Utilities. Follow the institutional outflows from megacaps.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: Market context is poor. A-grade setups are non-existent. Best to sit on hands and observe.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Breakdowns in TSLA and GOOGL are on massive volume, confirming distribution. Avoid trying to catch these falling knives.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: Respect stops. The market has shown its hand to the downside. Do not fight the tape.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: No clean breakouts. The episodic pivot is happening to the downside in former leaders. Avoid longs until the market proves itself.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: Stay defensive. The tape is heavy with distribution in key leadership stocks. Wait for volatility to subside and a new trend to emerge. Raising cash and tightening stops on any existing longs is prudent.

standout.thesis: TSLA is breaking down on massive volume after earnings and guidance on heavy AI spending spooked investors. This represents a major shift in sentiment for a market leader.

yesterdayReactions.result: missed profit estimates, warned of high capex
beat estimates but raised capex forecast significantly

alert: High volatility expected. Market reacting to tech spending fears and new US tariffs.

news.headline: Investors Zero In on Runaway Tech Spending, Putting Dent in AI Trade
Trump Unveils New Tariffs Designed to Withstand Legal Scrutiny, hitting 60 countries with 10-12.5% duties
Asian markets close sharply lower after U.S. imposes new tariffs
Oil prices fall back below $100 a barrel but geopolitical tensions remain high
Tesla shares dip more than 14% after the company reported mixed Q2 financial results
